title: Else Statements
description: Provide an alternative when the 'if' condition fails.

slides.title: The else Statement 🔀
slides.content:
What happens if the condition in an `if` statement is `false`? The block is skipped entirely!

If you want a fallback action, you use the `else` statement. It specifies a block of code to be executed if the condition is `false`.

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Since time is NOT less than 18, it skips to the else block.

quizData.question: What is the output of the preceding code snippet?
quizData.explanation: Because 20 < 18 is false, the execution goes to the else block, printing 'Good evening.'.

slides.title: No Condition Need 🛑
slides.content:
Notice that the `else` block does NOT have its own condition (`()` parentheses).

It is simply a catch-all block that runs only when the `if` condition fails.

quizData.question: Can you have an else block without a preceding if block?
quizData.explanation: False. An else block MUST immediately follow an if (or else if) block. It cannot exist on its own.
